question: Among DC wards 7 and 8, identify which ward had the higher average 311 service requests from 2019 through 2020. Who represents that ward on the DC Council? This council member earned a graduate degree from a university whose flagship building is a National Historic Landmark. Among the founder of the university and the person the flagship building is named after, in what year was the earlier-born individual born? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact:
import io
import json
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
source = "datagov/311-city-service-requests-in-2019/files/data.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
data = json.load(io.TextIOWrapper(obj["Body"], encoding="utf-8"))
df = pd.json_normalize(data["features"])
ward_col = "properties.WARD"
result = (
    df[df[ward_col].isin(["7", "8", 7, 8])]
    .groupby(ward_col)
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="request_count")
)
result[ward_col] = result[ward_col].astype(str)
result = result.sort_values("request_count", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
answer = [f"Ward {row[ward_col]} ({row['request_count']:,})" for _, row in result.iterrows()]
1.subquestion: For 2019, how many 311 service requests were recorded in Ward 7 and Ward 8?

import io
import json
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config
source = "datagov/311-city-service-requests-in-2020/files/data.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
data = json.load(io.TextIOWrapper(obj["Body"], encoding="utf-8"))
df = pd.json_normalize(data["features"])
ward_col = "properties.WARD"
result = (
    df[df[ward_col].isin(["7", "8", 7, 8])]
    .groupby(ward_col)
    .size()
    .reset_index(name="request_count")
)
result[ward_col] = result[ward_col].astype(str)
result = result.sort_values("request_count", ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
answer = [f"Ward {row[ward_col]} ({row['request_count']:,})" for _, row in result.iterrows()]
